head: 8d7f85f
host: meh (24 cores, 125 GiB RAM, NixOS, kernel 6.12.83)

comparison.note: On meh (125 GiB RAM, 24 cores) the v1.5.0 mmap path delivers a statistically irrelevant 0.15 ms p50 improvement: both modes converge to ~26.7 ms because the entire 1.5 GB index fits trivially in OS page cache, making the buffer-manager read path effectively free. The 60-90 ms regime that motivated Phase R-3 was an arnold-class (i9-12900H, 31 GiB RAM) phenomenon.

verdict_explanation: Phase S delivers no measurable warm-scan win on meh because the bottleneck Phase S targets (buffer-manager copy of the 1.5 GB static regions on a host where they don't fit shared_buffers) is fully masked by the OS page cache when free RAM is plentiful. With 125 GiB total / ~76 GiB free, the entire 1.5 GB index sits comfortably in the kernel page cache; the 'pread -> shared_buffers slot -> chain Vec' path that Phase S replaces costs ~0 because pread hits warm OS cache and the buffer-manager work is pure memcpy. mmap eliminates that memcpy in principle, but the savings (~0.1 ms p50) are below measurement noise on a 26 ms baseline. Phase S is expected to deliver the predicted win on hosts where free RAM is constrained relative to the index size (the original arnold profile: 31 GiB RAM, 1.5 GB index, plus other working sets) -- a re-bench on arnold remains the definitive validation. The U-2 numbers do confirm v1.5.0 is at-worst neutral relative to v1.4.x on a generously-RAMed host: no regression.

methodology.primary: Single warm psql session per config (one persistent backend). 2 untimed warmup queries against qid=1 (the first re-pays the per-backend init cost: Lloyd-Max codebook + blocked-layout repack + relfile rotation read; the second confirms cache-warm steady-state). Then 50 timed queries, qid=1..50, each routed through bench_one_query_tv(qid) which times an ORDER BY (emb::real[]::turbovec.vector) OPERATOR(turbovec.<=>) q LIMIT 10 via clock_timestamp() inside a plpgsql wrapper. Same shape as Phase J / Phase O-3 / Phase R-3.
methodology.config_isolation: Each config runs in a fresh psql connection so the per-backend cache starts empty and the new mmap_static_blocked GUC value is observed on the warmup1 cache-miss install. Verified by SHOW turbovec.mmap_static_blocked emitting 'on' / 'off' in each session log.
methodology.shared_buffers: 512MB (matches arnold Phase R-3 setup; smaller than the 1.5 GB index)

raw_observations.bimodal_distribution: Both configs show bimodal latencies: ~25-38 queries cluster at ~26.5-26.7 ms (fast), ~12-14 queries cluster at ~60 ms (slow), with a small middle tail. The p50 captures the fast cluster. The p95 captures the slow cluster, suggesting query-set-dependent variance (some query vectors trigger a cheaper search-k pruning path than others).
raw_observations.warmup1_ms: Pays per-backend cache-miss build (Lloyd-Max codebook + blocked-layout repack + rotation matrix read). 4.19 s mmap=on, 3.15 s mmap=off.
raw_observations.warmup2_ms: Cache-warm steady-state on qid=1 specifically. ~50-60 ms in both configs (qid=1 happens to be a 'slow' query).

u1_cache_diagnosis.test: Built v1.5.0 with a temporary atomic-counter tracepoint in cache::lookup() exposing cache_stats() / cache_stats_reset() SQL functions. Loaded a 2000 x 64-d random unit vector corpus, built the turbovec 4-bit index, and ran 50 separate ORDER BY queries against it inside a single warm psql session.
u1_cache_diagnosis.interpretation: The Phase S agent's hypothesis that the per-backend Arc<RwLock<IdMapIndex>> cache misses on every warm scan is wrong. The cache hits 100 % of the time after the first install. The HashMap::insert hot symbol the Phase S agent saw in perf was almost certainly the one-shot finalise_from_inner build during the cold/warmup1 cache-miss install (~1 M HashMap::insert calls for the 1 M-vector id-map build), amortised over the 50-query sample but not actually running per query. The warm-scan bottleneck on hardware where it is observable is therefore NOT the cache lookup; it is downstream (search kernel, ORDER BY recheck heap fetches, or buffer-manager static-region reads on RAM-constrained hosts).
u1_cache_diagnosis.tracepoint_status: Tracepoint code reverted before the v1.5.0 sweep build; not committed.

reproducer: /scratch/pg_turbovec-bench/u2_run.sh on meh (sources env.sh; uses bench_one_query_tv from benches/scripts/bench_million_setup.sql)
